_about: CANONICAL LAYOUT (Phase 1 visual-QA, Stage 2 — issues #6/#8/#11). The single documented source of truth for where everything sits, in WORLD metres. This is a REFERENCE/spec, not a file the app loads: the running app resolves the same arrangement at runtime from scene.json + the selected prop variant + the item library (see `sources`), and BOTH the Motion Lab and production use that one path, so they are identical. Read these numbers (or call __motionLab.layoutSnapshot() live) when authoring motions so a hand reaches the REAL prop, not a magic number. Regenerate after any layout change: __motionLab.layoutSnapshot().

resolvedFrom.variants: public/models/props/props.variants.json (registry defaults: desk=desk_office_metal, chair=chair_office, laptop=pc_laptop_modern — identical in production where there is no localStorage)

world.facing: +Z (character vrm.scene.rotation.y = PI). Therefore the character's RIGHT side is world -x (screen-left in the front camera) and her LEFT side is world +x.

desk.note: work surface; keyboard/typing happens here in front of her

chair.note: office chair behind/under her; seat surface ~0.45-0.47, backrest behind (-z)

laptop.note: monitor she looks at; keyboard/base is the near (smaller-z) part of the laptop on the desk

cupRest.note: CANONICAL right-side rest (issue #6/#7/#8). Placed within the right hand's measured reach envelope (~[-0.25,0.79,0.17] at desk height); -0.52 was beyond reach and caused the old cup warp.

characterIdleBones._note: world positions at the STANDING idle rest pose (no posture). Sitting postures lower hips ~0.2 (sit_pc_neutral hipsOffset [0,-0.2,0.05]).

authoringTargets._note: derived helpers for pose authoring; verify live with layoutSnapshot() at the frozen pose.

typingHandZone.note: both hands on the laptop keyboard; right hand can drift to the mouse on the right (-x)
